description_long: This is a large, L-shaped filing cabinet or archival shelf unit, featuring two main sections joined at a right angle. The longer section consists of multiple evenly spaced vertical dividers, each holding a row of flat, rectangular items that resemble files, folders, or vinyl records. The shorter perpendicular section is similarly divided and filled, suggesting a modular design for efficient storage and organization. The exterior is a light beige or off-white color, with the dividers and contents providing a subtle contrast in tone. The open-top design allows for easy access to the stored items, and the overall construction appears to be made of wood or laminated particleboard. This type of unit is commonly found in libraries, archives, or office environments for organized storage and retrieval.
description: A large, L-shaped open-top filing cabinet or archival shelf with multiple vertical dividers and rows of flat items, finished in a light beige color. Designed for organized storage and easy access, it is likely made of wood or laminate.
description_view_0: The L-shape is clearly visible, showing both the long and short sections filled with rows of flat items and vertical dividers.
description_view_1: The shorter section is in the foreground, emphasizing the depth and the arrangement of dividers and contents within both sections.
description_view_2: A straight-on view of the longer section, highlighting the uniformity of the dividers and the full length of the storage unit.
description_view_3: The shorter section is most prominent, with the long section extending away, showing the open-top design and internal organization.
